THESIS: The night is the product. The video is the receipt. Every setup doubles as a vertical cut, engineered to stand alone as a Reel and assemble into the 3:12 hero film. We capture once; we deliver many.
CAPTURE_MODEL: One night, three locations, four-person crew. No second unit. The hero is the assembly.
CONCEPT_LEDE: The film splits at the drop. Rehearsed · stolen.
CONCEPT_A_BODY: One block, one take, one hour before the light dies. Kilo walks the same hundred meters while the city resets around him. Every pass, something is wrong in a new way: a stranger too still, a bus that never arrives, the same dog twice.
CONCEPT_B_BODY: The video becomes the found footage. Security cams, dash cams, a stranger's vertical phone video. The song assembles itself out of what the night recorded. Aspect ratios collide; timestamp burn-ins tell the real story.
BEAT_LEDE: Cut to the song. The intro holds underwater until the vocal punches through; the filter releases at the drop.
BEAT_01_VISUAL: Black. Title card: KILO NOVA. Streetlight flickers on.
BEAT_02_VISUAL: THE LOOP block. Same street, three passes, wrongness compounding.
BEAT_03_AUDIO: Full volume. Filter off. Drums hit clean.
BEAT_03_VISUAL: Hard cut to security-cam angle, timestamp burning in.
BEAT_04_VISUAL: Found-footage chain: dash cam, phone vertical, ATM cam.
BEAT_05_AUDIO: Tail. City ambience swallows the beat.
BEAT_05_VISUAL: Return to the loop street, empty now. Title card.
SHORTS_LEDE: Every setup is a standalone vertical. Three from the loop block shown here.
S1_FRAME: Full body under a single sodium lamp, rest of street black
S1_MOVE: Kilo walks into the pool of light, stops
S2_FRAME: Identical framing to C1, details changed
S2_MOVE: Same walk, stranger now in background
S2_SFX: Footsteps doubled, slightly out of sync
S3_MOVE: Kilo turns to camera for the first time
S3_FX: Hard cut to black on the drop
TALENT_01_BODY: Direct, owned, never apologetic. Kilo looks at the camera like the street belongs to him.
TALENT_02_BODY: One outfit the whole night. The continuity IS the story; the cameras change, he does not.
TALENT_03_BODY: Sweat-real by the leak block. No touch-ups after the drop; degradation is the look.
GRAM_01_BODY: Low-pass riser (C1) ↔ filter release at the drop (C3).
GRAM_02_BODY: Loop block = one clean lens. Leak block = only cameras that exist in the world (security, dash, phone).
GRAM_03_BODY: Whip-pan · speed-ramp · hard-cut-on-timestamp. Three modes, rotated.
SIGNATURE: The streetlight flicker. It opens the film, marks every loop reset, and is the last thing alive in the outro.
LOC_LEDE: Three locations, one night, tracked to available light and city permits.
LOC_01_NOTE: Hundred-meter block · sodium lamps · permit locked
LOC_02_NOTE: Dash-cam and phone coverage · echo for live vocal pass
LOC_03_NOTE: Security-cam angle owned · fluorescent flicker practical
LOC_04_NOTE: Outro plate only · tripod · skyline behind
GRADE_LEDE: Two grades, one night: clean neutral for the loop, degraded sodium for the leak. The drop is the seam.
LIGHT_A_BODY: Available sodium + one hidden bounce. Locked white balance so every pass matches; wrongness comes from blocking, not grade.
LIGHT_B_BODY: Whatever the source camera would see. No movie lights in leak frames; fluorescents, headlights, and phone torches only.
VFX_NOTE: Minimal. Timestamp burn-ins, one split-diopter, speed ramps on the climax reel only. No comped elements.
SCHED_NOTE: All dates locked with the label; permits confirmed.
SCHED_01_WHAT: Pre-prod paper · shot list · call sheets · permits
SCHED_02_WHAT: Location scout-walks · loop-block rehearsal on street
SCHED_SHOOT_WHAT: SHOOT NIGHT · ONE NIGHT · THREE LOCATIONS + ROOFTOP MOVE
SCHED_06_WHAT: Grade + master · all deliverables rendered + handoff
